title: Coarse-to-fine geolocalization: a shared conv field ranks the region, a shared local head regresses the offset
hypothesis: Four straight reverts (36-39) all attacked the SAME output parameterization the project has used since round 2: a probability FIELD over map cells, decoded by soft-argmax, whether scored by a flat 1,024-independent-row table (2-38) or exp 39's coordinate-generated embedding table (still reverted, 1103.28 vs 743.07 m). Every one of those attempts kept the SAME shape of decode: one soft-argmax pass has to simultaneously (a) rank the true cell above every distant lookalike and (b) deliver metre-level precision, at whatever single grid resolution it commits to. The project's own diagnostic probes (runs/_probe_iter12_field.py, project memory champion-failure-mode-probes) show why that is two incompatible jobs at once: at 1,024 cells (~217 m/cell) the true cell is top-1 only ~2% of the time despite being in the top-20 33-58% of the time -- ranking that many near-identical candidates is the wall, not precision, since a coarser grid should be far easier to rank correctly (distant lookalike districts are usually NOT in the same coarse region as the truth). But a coarser grid alone destroys the precision the 20 m target needs. No experiment in 39 rounds has ever separated these two jobs into two stages. This is a genuine whole-pipeline pivot, not a parameterization tweak within the same one-shot decode: the field itself becomes a coarse, heavily parameter-shared, fully CONVOLUTIONAL grid (a 1x1 conv read directly off the trunk's own 8x8 spatial feature map -- the trunk's own translation structure DOES the parameter sharing, instead of either 1,024 free-standing rows or exp 39's Fourier-MLP generator, which still had to learn spatial structure abstractly), and a SEPARATE, cheap, shared fine-offset head -- reading the trunk's finer stride-8 tap -- regresses a per-cell (dx, dy) correction so the coarse cell's fixed center is nudged to the true location. Point regression was tried twice before (exp 1/2's single point, exp 37's K=8 hypotheses) and both lost specifically because nothing had resolved WHICH place first, so the regressor had to solve ranking and precision at once and collapsed toward the map centroid on ambiguous crops. Here the coarse field resolves ranking first (an easier 64-way problem); the offset regressor only ever has to solve a small, unimodal, well-conditioned local problem within whichever cell won -- a materially different setup from both prior regression failures.
method: Replace TinyLocNet's single flat 1024-way field (model/model.py) with a two-stage coarse+fine field: (1) the trunk's existing stride-16 8x8 feature grid is read by a 1x1 conv (2 output channels: bright/dark expert, blended by the existing brightness gate) directly INTO an 8x8=64-cell coarse field -- no flattening through a large Linear layer, the conv IS the field, so nearby coarse cells structurally share features by construction. (2) A NEW stride-8 tap (24ch @ 16x16, exactly 2x the stride-16 grid's resolution) is reshaped into per-coarse-cell 2x2 local blocks and read by ONE small shared conv head (identical weights at all 64 positions) producing a per-cell (Δu, Δv, logσ) correction. (3) Decode becomes a DSNT-style soft-argmax using PER-CELL fine-corrected centers (fixed grid center + that cell's own predicted offset) instead of fixed grid centers -- still fully differentiable, still decode-equals-what-the-loss-optimizes (no new argmax/gather anywhere in the exported graph). (4) Training signal becomes a joint loss: coarse Gaussian-smoothed CE over the 64-way field + a Huber offset-regression term gathered at the ground-truth cell only (a training-time-only gather, not exported) + the existing L2-on-decode and confidence BCE. (5) Confidence combines the coarse field's shape (as today) with the fine field's own predicted log-variance. (6) The confusability-weighted sampler (exp 35, kept) is recomputed at the new 8x8 granularity instead of 32x32. (7) The freshly-initialized offset head gets a 2-epoch LR warmup before joining the shared cosine schedule. This deletes the old loc_logits/dark_logits/layout_squeeze Linear layers entirely (~625k params) and replaces them with a tiny 1x1 conv (~100 params) plus a tiny shared offset conv head (~3k params) -- a large net reduction in output-side parameter count, i.e. far MORE sharing, not less, directly targeting the 'cannot fit its own training locations' signature diagnosed since round 20/25.
expected_outcome: If splitting ranking from precision is the fix, the worst-case median error improves from the champion's 743.07 m to roughly 480-680 m (8-35%), because a 64-way ranking problem should be far more tractable than the diagnosed 2%-top-1-of-1024 problem (distant lookalike cells are usually outside the true coarse cell entirely), while the fine offset regressor only has to solve a small, unimodal ~875 m local problem once the coarse cell is right. Mechanism-level signature to check post-hoc: a matched-protocol rerun of the iter-12 field probe should show coarse top-1 rate rise well above the champion's ~2% (of 1024) baseline -- e.g. >15% of 64 -- and train-split vs eval-split medians should FINALLY diverge (train dropping measurably below eval) instead of the tied ~700-1000 m both have shown since exp 20/25, direct evidence the much-more-parameter-shared coarse field can fit training locations the flat table could not. Falsification forks: (a) if coarse top-1 rate does NOT rise substantially and the metric is flat/regresses, the wall is not ranking-candidate-count but raw descriptor information content -- redirecting the next iteration at trunk/descriptor capacity, independent of parameterization, a genuinely new diagnosis; (b) if coarse top-1 rises clearly but the primary metric does not improve, the fine offset regressor is not transferring -- a descriptor-content gap specific to the FINE tap, pointing at enriching the local window (wider than 2x2, or a deeper stride-8 tap) rather than touching the coarse field again; (c) if the metric regresses sharply, check whether 8x8 is simply too coarse to separate confusable lookalike districts before the offset head can recover precision -- arguing for an intermediate resolution (e.g. 16x16=256) rather than abandoning the two-stage idea.
eli5: Every past attempt asked one system to do two hard jobs at once: figure out roughly WHERE a photo was taken among 1,024 tiny map tiles, AND pinpoint the exact spot -- in a single step. The diagnosis says the 'which tile' part is the part that keeps failing (the model narrows it down but usually doesn't pick the right one out of many similar-looking tiles). So this experiment splits the job in two: first, a much simpler 64-tile guess (roomier tiles, easier to get right, and computed straight off the camera model's own picture-shaped map instead of a giant independent answer sheet). Second, a small dedicated helper then nudges that guess to the exact spot within the winning tile -- a much easier fine-tuning problem once you already know the neighborhood. It's like first picking the right city block, then a second glance to find the exact doorway, instead of trying to name the doorway out of a thousand candidates in one look.

stages.name: Camera frame
stages.detail: 128×128 px aerial crop, one of 6 simulated lighting conditions

stages.name: Rotation fan
stages.detail: the crop is copied into its four 90° turns and all four copies travel through the same network side by side; the C4 vote now averages BOTH the coarse field logits and the per-cell fine offset predictions across the four views (previously only logits)

stages.name: Feature extractor
stages.detail: ImageNet-pretrained MobileNetV3-Small, now read at TWO depths: the existing stride-16 grid (8×8×48) for the coarse field, plus a NEW stride-8 tap (16×16×24) for the fine offset field

stages.name: Coarse localization field
stages.detail: the flat 1,024-independent-row template table is DELETED; a 1×1 conv reads the trunk's own 8×8 spatial grid directly into an 8×8=64-cell field (~875 m/cell) -- two brightness-gated experts (day/night) blended exactly as before, but as tiny shared convs instead of large Linear layers

stages.name: Lighting gate
stages.detail: unchanged mechanism (tiny MLP over brightness + GAP descriptor -> sigmoid blend weight) now blends the two 1×1-conv experts' spatial grids instead of two flat logit vectors

stages.name: Fine offset field
stages.detail: NEW stage: one small conv head, applied identically (shared weights) to all 64 coarse cells, reads each cell's own 2×2 block of the stride-8 tap and predicts a local (Δu, Δv, logσ) correction

stages.name: Decode
stages.detail: soft-argmax (β-sharpened, unchanged philosophy) now weights PER-CELL fine-corrected centers (fixed grid center + that cell's own predicted offset) instead of fixed grid centers -- still no argmax/gather in the exported graph

stages.name: Confidence
stages.detail: combines the coarse field's shape (sharpened-peak mass, entropy, sharp-vs-unsharp decode gap, as before) with the fine field's own probability-weighted log-variance -- below a tuned bar it still says 'no fix'

stages.name: Training data
stages.detail: the confusability-weighted sampler (exp 35, kept) is recomputed at the new 8×8 coarse-cell granularity instead of 32×32 -- same mechanism, new map of which cells are confusable

stages.name: Training signal
stages.detail: joint loss: coarse Gaussian-smoothed CE over the 64-way field + a Huber offset-regression term gathered at the ground-truth cell only (training-time gather, never exported) + L2 on the final decode + confidence BCE -- previously a single-field CE + L2 + conf BCE

stages.name: Training schedule
stages.detail: the freshly-initialized fine-offset head gets a 2-epoch LR warmup before joining the shared per-step cosine anneal (kept from round 25) -- previously one uniform schedule for all params

implementation_brief:
Files: model/model.py (architecture + loss) and model/train.py (only the constant import and compute_cell_weights/crop_cell, which already reference GRID_K generically -- no logic changes needed there beyond the constant's new value and meaning). FROZEN CONTRACTS to restate and preserve exactly: train.py's CLI (`python -m model.train --area <area> --out-dir <dir> [--epochs N]`), and model.py's export_onnx contract -- ONNX takes 1x3x128x128 float32 in [0,1] and returns [[u, v, conf]] with u,v normalized map coords in [0,1] and conf in [0,1]; opset 17, dynamo=False, exactly as today.

1) model/model.py constants: rename nothing required -- reuse GRID_K but change its meaning/value from 32 (1024-cell field) to 8 (64-cell COARSE field); this keeps train.py's `from model.model import GRID_K` and its GRID_K-parameterized compute_cell_weights/crop_cell working unchanged, just operating on 8x8 blocks instead of 32x32. Add FINE_MULT = 2 (stride-8 tap is exactly 2x the stride-16 grid's linear resolution, i.e. FINE_MULT*GRID_K per side -- verify this empirically with the same zero-tensor probe pattern _build_pretrained_trunk already uses, do not hardcode without checking).

2) Split the trunk: currently `self.features = _build_pretrained_trunk()` is one nn.Sequential covering features[:9]. Change _build_pretrained_trunk (or add a second helper) to expose TWO sub-modules: `self.features_early` (the prefix ending at the stride-8 activation, 16x16 spatial, 24ch per existing docstring/exp-29 precedent) and `self.features_late` (continuing from there through the existing features[:9] endpoint, 8x8 spatial, 48ch). Load the SAME pretrained state dict, sliced across both halves so behavior is bit-identical to today's single-pass features(x) when composed (features_late(features_early(x)) == features(x)). Probe both halves' output shapes with a zero tensor exactly as the constructor already does for the full trunk, and use the probed channel counts / spatial sizes -- do not assume exact indices without checking; the module boundary is wherever the trunk's spatial resolution transitions from 16x16 to 8x8 for a 128x128 input.

3) Forward pass (replaces the current logits4 pipeline):
   - `x4 = _c4_stack(x)` (unchanged).
   - `early4 = self.features_early((x4 - norm_mean) / norm_std)`  # [4N, Ce, 16, 16]
   - `late4 = self.features_late(early4)`  # [4N, Cl, 8, 8]  (Cl=48 as today)
   - `f4 = late4.mean(dim=(2,3))`  # [4N, Cl] GAP descriptor, feeds gate + conf head exactly as today
   - Coarse field: `bright4 = self.coarse_bright(late4)` and `dark4 = self.coarse_dark(late4)`, each an `nn.Conv2d(Cl, 1, 1)` -> [4N,1,8,8]. Compute the gate `g4` exactly as today (same brightness+GAP MLP, same sigmoid). `coarse_logits4 = ((1-g4)*bright4 + g4*dark4).flatten(1)`  # [4N, 64], flatten is row-major over (gy,gx) which MUST match _grid_centers' `gy*k+gx` convention -- verify by construction, do not assume.
   - Fine field: reshape `early4` [4N,Ce,16,16] into per-coarse-cell 2x2 blocks: `local = early4.view(N4, Ce, GRID_K, FINE_MULT, GRID_K, FINE_MULT).permute(0,2,4,1,3,5).reshape(N4, GRID_K, GRID_K, Ce*FINE_MULT*FINE_MULT).permute(0,3,1,2)`  # [4N, Ce*4, 8, 8], channel-first for conv. Apply a small shared head `self.offset_head = nn.Sequential(nn.Conv2d(Ce*FINE_MULT*FINE_MULT, 32, 1), nn.ReLU(), nn.Conv2d(32, 3, 1))` -> raw [4N,3,8,8]. `du4 = (0.5/GRID_K) * torch.tanh(raw[:,0])`, `dv4 = (0.5/GRID_K) * torch.tanh(raw[:,1])` (bounds the offset to +/- half a coarse cell in normalized uv units), `logvar4 = raw[:,2]`. Flatten all three to [4N,64] with the SAME row-major (gy,gx) convention as the coarse field.
   - C4 vote: average `coarse_logits4`, `du4`, `dv4`, `logvar4` each reshaped to [4,N,64] over dim 0 (blind average across the 4 rotated views, same mechanism the existing code already uses for logits -- do not attempt geometric un-rotation of the grid this round, that is a separate, riskier follow-up).
   - Per-cell fine-corrected centers: `cell_u, cell_v = _grid_centers(GRID_K)` (unchanged helper, now called with k=8) broadcast to [1,64]; `center_u = cell_u[None,:] + du`, `center_v = cell_v[None,:] + dv` (both [N,64]).
   - Decode (replaces the old fixed-buffer soft-argmax): `p = softmax(DECODE_BETA*logits, dim=1)`; `u = (p*center_u).sum(1,keepdim=True)`; `v = (p*center_v).sum(1,keepdim=True)` (same for the unsharpened `p1/u1/v1` pair used by the confidence gap term). `logvar = (p.detach()*logvar).sum(1,keepdim=True)` (probability-weighted mean log-variance, detached probability weights so no extra gradient path opens here beyond what the existing conf_head detach discipline already allows).
   - Confidence: same conf_head MLP as today, but its input now also concatenates the detached `logvar` alongside the existing [f.detach(), peak, ent, gap] -- widen conf_head's first Linear's in_features by 1 accordingly.
   - `out = torch.cat([u, v, conf], dim=1)`; return `(out, logits)` when return_logits, exactly as today.

4) loss_fn: needs the ground-truth coarse cell index and within-cell offset. Given `target_uv` [N,2]: `gx = (target_uv[:,0]*GRID_K).floor().clamp(0, GRID_K-1).long()`, `gy = (target_uv[:,1]*GRID_K).floor().clamp(0, GRID_K-1).long()`, `cell_idx = gy*GRID_K + gx`. Coarse CE: identical Gaussian-smoothed cross-entropy the current code already computes via `_grid_centers(GRID_K)` and TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS (reconsider the constant's value now that cells are ~4x larger in linear size than before -- a similar effective smoothing radius in meters means TARGET_SIGMA_CELLS should likely shrink, e.g. to ~0.75-1.0; pick a defensible value and note it in train_info.json, this is a legitimate re-derivation not a hyperparameter sweep). Fine offset loss: gather the predicted `(du, dv)` (pre-vote-averaged per-view is fine, or the C4-voted version -- use the voted [N,64] tensors for simplicity) at `cell_idx` via `torch.gather`, compare with Huber/SmoothL1 loss against the true continuous offset `(target_uv[:,0] - cell_u[cell_idx] , target_uv[:,1] - cell_v[cell_idx])` -- this gather is training-loss-only, never exported, so it's fine to use dynamic indexing here even though the exported forward() avoids it. Total loss = coarse_ce + fine_offset_huber + coord_loss (L2 on final decoded (u,v), unchanged) + 0.3*conf_loss (unchanged BCE target/weight) -- pick a reasonable weight for fine_offset_huber (e.g. 1.0) and log it.

5) model/train.py: only two changes. (a) After building the model, split its parameters into three LR groups instead of two for the first 2 epochs: trunk (1e-4, unchanged), existing/shared head params (1e-3, unchanged), and the NEW `model.offset_head` params at a lower warmup LR (e.g. 3e-4) for epochs 0-1, then merge them into the normal 1e-3 head group for the remaining epochs -- reconstruct the optimizer's param groups (or just use a per-group LR multiplier schedule) so the fresh conv head doesn't get destabilizing early gradients alongside an already-fine-tuned pretrained trunk. (b) No changes needed to sample_epoch, prepare_realizations, calibrate_conf_shift, or compute_cell_weights/crop_cell -- they already parameterize on GRID_K generically and will operate correctly at k=8.

6) Keep export_onnx and estimate_position byte-identical -- the model's forward() contract (input/output shapes) is unchanged, only what happens inside it. Sanity-check param count and a dry export before considering the brief complete: expect the exported artifact to SHRINK substantially versus the champion (deleted ~625k-param Linear heads, added ~3k-param shared conv heads) -- comfortably clears the 4 MiB gate without needing int8; latency should also improve (small convs vs large FC).
